comment: World flips (Q10, §3), full arc ending 1 of 2 — the rebuild. Authored-dark: the rebuilt span is placed `enabled:false`, so it is dark (not instanced) until a stage enables it, while the broken span is instanced. The choice to rebuild latches immediately, but the WORK takes three days: the terminal gates on `since` its rebuild-decision, so the flip LANDS DURING advance_hours catch-up (replay_advance runs the span twice and demands the flipped world.group.* replay bit-identically — the flip's catch-up law asserted). When it lands the broken group flips off and the rebuilt group flips on: the far bank the sequel walks through opens. Same record as brook_bridge_left.test.json; the other ending.

stages.journal: Word came that the brook bridge gave way in the night.
I stood at the broken span. The far bank waited across the water.
We decided to rebuild. The timber came up from the mill.
Three days on, the new bridge held our weight. The far bank opens again.
We left it broken; the ford will have to do.
